name: GetPoint
summary: Used to interactively get a point.

methods.name: AcceptColor
methods.summary: If you want to allow the user to be able to type in a color r,g,b or name during GetPoint.Get(), GetObject::GetObjects(), etc., then call AcceptColor(true) before calling GetPoint()/GetObject(). If the user chooses to type in a color, then the result code GetResult.Color is returned and you can use RhinoGet.Color() to get the value of the color. If the get accepts points, then the user will not be able to type in r,g,b colors but will be able to type color names.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if user is able to type a color.

methods.name: AcceptCustomMessage
methods.summary: (Inherited from GetBaseClass.)

params.name: enable
params.doc: [Missing <param name="enable"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetBaseClass.AcceptCustomMessage(System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: AcceptEnterWhenDone
methods.summary: There are instances of RhinoGet that prompt with "Press Enter when Done." yet do not call AcceptNothing(). On the Mac, these instances need an additional call to AcceptEnterWhenDone() so the GetPointOptions dialog can correctly enable the Done button.

params.name: enable
params.doc: [Missing <param name="enable"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetBaseClass.AcceptEnterWhenDone(System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: AcceptNothing
methods.summary: If you want to allow the user to be able to press enter in order to skip selecting a something in GetPoint.Get(), GetObject::GetObjects(), etc., then call AcceptNothing( true ) beforehand.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if user is able to press enter in order to skip selecting.

methods.name: AcceptNumber
methods.signature: public void AcceptNumber(bool enable, bool acceptZero)
methods.summary: If you want to allow the user to be able to type in a number during GetPoint.Get(), GetObject::GetObjects(), etc., then call AcceptNumber() beforehand. If the user chooses to type in a number, then the result code GetResult.Number is returned and you can use RhinoGet.Number() to get the value of the number. If you are using GetPoint and you want "0" to return (0,0,0) instead of the number zero, then set acceptZero = false.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if user is able to type a number.

params.name: acceptZero
params.doc: If you are using GetPoint and you want "0" to return (0,0,0) instead of the number zero, then set acceptZero = false.

methods.name: AcceptPoint
methods.summary: If you want to allow the user to be able to type in a point then call AcceptPoint(true) before calling GetPoint()/GetObject(). If the user chooses to type in a number, then the result code GetResult.Point is returned and you can use RhinoGet.Point() to get the value of the point.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if user is able to type in a point.

methods.name: AcceptString
methods.summary: If you want to allow the user to be able to type in a string during GetPoint.Get(), GetObject::GetObjects(), etc., then call AcceptString(true) before calling GetPoint()/GetObject(). If the user chooses to type in a string, then the result code GetResult.String is returned and you can use RhinoGet.String() to get the value of the string.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if user is able to type a string.

methods.name: AcceptUndo
methods.summary: If you want to allow the user to have an 'undo' option in GetPoint.Get(), GetObject.GetObjects(), etc., then call AcceptUndo(true) beforehand.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if user is able to choose the 'Undo' option.

methods.name: AddConstructionPoint
methods.summary: Adds a point to the list of construction points.
methods.remarks: Construction points are like snap points except that they get snapped to even when point osnap is off. Typically, there are only a few construction points while there can be many snap points. For example, when polylines are drawn the start point is a construction point and the other points are snap points.

params.name: point
params.doc: A point to be added.

returns.doc: Total number of construction points.

methods.name: AddConstructionPoints
methods.summary: Adds points to the list of construction points.
methods.remarks: Construction points are like snap points except that they get snapped to even when point osnap is off. Typically, there are only a few construction points while there can be many snap points. For example, when polylines are drawn the start point is a construction point and the other points are snap points.

params.name: points
params.doc: An array of points to be added.

returns.doc: Total number of construction points.

methods.name: AddSnapPoint
methods.summary: Adds a point to the list of osnap points.
methods.remarks: When point osnap is enabled, GetPoint will snap to points in the Rhino model. If you want the user to be able to snap to additional points, then use GetPoint::AddSnapPoints to specify the locations of these additional points.

params.name: point
params.doc: A point.

returns.doc: Total number of snap points.

methods.name: AddSnapPoints
methods.summary: Adds points to the list of osnap points.
methods.remarks: When point osnap is enabled, GetPoint will snap to points in the Rhino model. If you want the user to be able to snap to additional points, then use GetPoint::AddSnapPoints to specify the locations of these additional points.

params.name: points
params.doc: An array of points to snap onto.

returns.doc: Total number of snap points.

methods.name: ClearCommandOptions
methods.summary: Clear all command options.

methods.name: ClearConstraints
methods.summary: Removes any explicit constraints added by calls to GetPoint::Constraint() and enable the built-in constraint options.

methods.name: ClearConstructionPoints
methods.summary: Remove all construction points.
methods.remarks: Construction points are like snap points except that they get snapped to even when point osnap is off. Typically, there are only a few construction points while there can be many snap points. For example, when polylines are drawn the start point is a construction point and the other points are snap points.

methods.name: ClearDefault
methods.summary: Clears any defaults set using SetDefaultPoint, SetDefaultNumber, SetDefaultString, or SetCommandPromptDefault.

methods.name: ClearSnapPoints
methods.summary: Remove all snap points.
methods.remarks: When point osnap is enabled, GetPoint will snap to points in the Rhino model. If you want the user to be able to snap to additional points, then use GetPoint::AddSnapPoints to specify the locations of these additional points.

methods.name: Color
methods.summary: Gets a color if Get*() returns GetResult.Color.

returns.doc: The color chosen by the user.

methods.name: CommandResult
methods.summary: Helper method for getting command result value from getter results.

returns.doc: The converted command result.

methods.name: Constrain(Arc)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on an arc.

params.name: arc
params.doc: An arc to use as constraint.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a circle.

params.doc: A circle to use as constraint.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a cylinder.

params.name: cylinder

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Line)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a line.

params.name: line

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Sphere)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a sphere.

params.name: sphere
params.doc: A sphere to use as constraint.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Curve, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Constrain(Curve curve, bool allowPickingPointOffObject)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a curve.

params.name: curve
params.doc: A curve to use as constraint.

params.name: allowPickingPointOffObject
params.doc: defines whether the point pick is allowed to happen off object. When false, a "no no" cursor is shown when the cursor is not on the object. When true, a normal point picking cursor is used and the marker is visible also when the cursor is not on the object.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Mesh, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Constrain(Mesh mesh, bool allowPickingPointOffObject)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a mesh.

params.name: mesh
params.doc: A mesh to use as constraint.

params.name: allowPickingPointOffObject
params.doc: defines whether the point pick is allowed to happen off object. When false, a "no no" cursor is shown when the cursor is not on the object. When true, a normal point picking cursor is used and the marker is visible also when the cursor is not on the object.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Plane, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Constrain(Plane plane, bool allowElevator)
methods.summary: constrain the picked point to lie on a plane.

params.name: plane
params.doc: A plane to use as constraint.

params.name: allowElevator
params.doc: true if elevator mode should be allowed at user request.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Point3d, Point3d)
methods.signature: public bool Constrain(Point3d from, Point3d to)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a line.

params.name: from
params.doc: The start point of constraint.

params.name: to
params.doc: The end point of constraint.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Surface, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Constrain(Surface surface, bool allowPickingPointOffObject)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a surface.

params.name: surface
params.doc: A surface to use as constraint.

params.name: allowPickingPointOffObject
params.doc: defines whether the point pick is allowed to happen off object. When false, a "no no" cursor is shown when the cursor is not on the object. When true, a normal point picking cursor is used and the marker is visible also when the cursor is not on the object.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: Constrain(Brep, Int32, Int32, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool Constrain(Brep brep, int wireDensity, int faceIndex, bool allowPickingPointOffObject)
methods.summary: Constrains the picked point to lie on a brep.

params.name: brep
params.doc: A brep to use as constraint.

params.name: wireDensity
params.doc: When wire_density<0, isocurve intersection snapping is turned off, when wire_density>=0, the value defines the isocurve density used for isocurve intersection snapping.

params.name: faceIndex
params.doc: When face_index <0, constrain to whole brep. When face_index >=0, constrain to individual face.

params.name: allowPickingPointOffObject
params.doc: defines whether the point pick is allowed to happen off object. When false, a "no no" cursor is shown when the cursor is not on the object. When true, a normal point picking cursor is used and the marker is visible also when the cursor is not on the object.

returns.doc: true if constraint could be applied.

methods.name: ConstrainDistanceFromBasePoint
methods.summary: Sets distance constraint from base point.
methods.remarks: If the base point is set and the distance from base point constraint is > 0, then the picked point is constrained to be this distance from the base point.

params.name: distance
params.doc: pass UnsetValue to clear this constraint. Pass 0.0 to disable the ability to set this constraint by typing a number during GetPoint.

methods.name: ConstrainToConstructionPlane
methods.summary: If enabled, the picked point is constrained to be on the active construction plane. If the base point is set, then the point is constrained to be on the plane that contains the base point and is parallel to the active construction plane. By default this constraint is enabled.

params.name: throughBasePoint
params.doc: true if the base point should be used as compulsory level reference.

returns.doc: If true and the base point is set, then the point is constrained to be on the plane parallel to the construction plane that passes through the base point, even when planar mode is off. If throughBasePoint is false, then the base point shift only happens if planar mode is on.

methods.name: ConstrainToTargetPlane
methods.summary: Constrains point to lie on a plane that is parallel to the viewing plane and passes through the view's target point.

methods.name: ConstrainToVirtualCPlaneIntersection
methods.summary: If enabled, the picked point is constrained to be on the intersection of the plane and the virtual CPlane going through the plane origin. If the planes are parallel, the constraint works just like planar constraint.

params.name: plane
params.doc: The plane used for the plane - virtual CPlane intersection.

returns.doc: true if the operation succeeded; false otherwise.

methods.name: CustomMessage
methods.summary: (Inherited from GetBaseClass.)

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetBaseClass.CustomMessage"]

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: (Inherited from GetBaseClass.)

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: (Inherited from GetBaseClass.)

params.name: disposing
params.doc: True to release both managed and unmanaged resources; false to release only unmanaged resources

methods.name: DrawLineFromPoint
methods.signature: public void DrawLineFromPoint(Point3d startPoint, bool showDistanceInStatusBar)
methods.summary: Use DrawLineFromPoint() if you want a dynamic line drawn from a point to the point being picked.
methods.remarks: Calling DrawLineFromPoint automatically enables drawing the line. Use EnableDrawLineFromPoint() to toggle the line drawing state.

params.name: startPoint
params.doc: The line is drawn from startPoint to the point being picked. If the base point has not been set, then it is set to startPoint.

params.name: showDistanceInStatusBar
params.doc: if true, the distance from the basePoint to the point begin picked is shown in the status bar.

methods.name: EnableCurveSnapArrow
methods.signature: public void EnableCurveSnapArrow(bool drawDirectionArrowAtSnapPoint, bool reverseArrow)
methods.summary: Controls display of the curve snap arrow icon.
methods.remarks: The tangent bar is drawn by GetPoint::DynamicDraw. If you override GetPoint::DynamicDraw, then you must call the base class function.

params.name: drawDirectionArrowAtSnapPoint
params.doc: true to draw arrow icon whenever GetPoint snaps to a curve.

params.name: reverseArrow
params.doc: true if arrow icon direction should be the reverse of the first derivative direction.

methods.name: EnableCurveSnapPerpBar
methods.signature: public void EnableCurveSnapPerpBar(bool drawPerpBarAtSnapPoint, bool drawEndPoints)
methods.summary: Controls display of the curve snap perpendicular bar icon.

params.name: drawPerpBarAtSnapPoint
params.doc: true to draw a tangent bar icon whenever GetPoint snaps to a curve.

params.name: drawEndPoints
params.doc: true to draw points at the end of the tangent bar.

methods.name: EnableCurveSnapTangentBar
methods.signature: public void EnableCurveSnapTangentBar(bool drawTangentBarAtSnapPoint, bool drawEndPoints)
methods.summary: Controls display of the curve snap tangent bar icon.
methods.remarks: The tangent bar is drawn by GetPoint::DynamicDraw. If you override GetPoint::DynamicDraw, then you must call the base class function.

params.name: drawTangentBarAtSnapPoint
params.doc: true to draw a tangent bar icon whenever GetPoint snaps to a curve.

params.name: drawEndPoints
params.doc: true to draw points at the end of the tangent bar.

methods.name: EnableDrawLineFromPoint
methods.summary: Controls drawing of dynamic a line from the start point.

params.name: enable
params.doc: if true, a dynamic line is drawn from the DrawLineFromPoint startPoint to the point being picked.

methods.name: EnableNoRedrawOnExit
methods.summary: The default functionality of the GetPoint operation is to perform a redraw on exit. Calling this function with true turns off automatic redraw at the end of GetPoint. May be needed in some commands for flicker free feedback. When set to true, the caller is responsible for cleaning up the screen after GetPoint.

params.name: noRedraw
params.doc: [Missing <param name="noRedraw"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.EnableNoRedrawOnExit(System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: EnableObjectSnapCursors
methods.summary: Enables or disables object snap cursors. By default, object snap cursors are enabled.

params.name: enable
params.doc: If true then object snap cursors (plus sign with "near", "end", etc.) are used when the point snaps to a object.

methods.name: EnableSnapToCurves
methods.summary: If you want GetPoint() to try to snap to curves when the mouse is near a curve (like the center point in the Circle command when the AroundCurve option is on), then enable the snap to curves option.

params.name: enable
params.doc: Whether points should be enabled.

methods.name: EnableTransparentCommands
methods.summary: Control the availability of transparent commands during the get.
methods.remarks: Some Rhino commands are "transparent" and can be run inside of other commands. Examples of transparent commands include the view manipulation commands like ZoomExtents, Top, etc., and the selection commands like SelAll, SelPoint, etc. By default transparent commands can be run during any get. If you want to disable this feature, then call EnableTransparentCommands(false) before calling GetString, GetPoint, GetObject, etc.

params.name: enable
params.doc: If true, then transparent commands can be run during the get. If false, then transparent commands cannot be run during the get.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: (Inherited from GetBaseClass.)

methods.name: Get()
methods.summary: After setting up options and so on, call GetPoint::Get to get a 3d point. The point is retrieved when the mouse goes down.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.Get"]

methods.name: Get(Boolean)
methods.summary: After setting up options and so on, call this method to get a 3d point.

params.name: onMouseUp
params.doc: If false, the point is returned when the left mouse button goes down. If true, the point is returned when the left mouse button goes up.

returns.doc: Point if the user chose a point; other enumeration value otherwise.

methods.name: Get(Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public GetResult Get(bool onMouseUp, bool get2DPoint)
methods.summary: After setting up options and so on, call this method to get a 2d or 3d point.

params.name: onMouseUp
params.doc: If false, the point is returned when the left mouse button goes down. If true, the point is returned when the left mouse button goes up.

params.name: get2DPoint
params.doc: If true then get a 2d point otherwise get a 2d point

returns.doc: Point if the user chose a 3d point; Point2d if the user chose a 2d point; other enumeration value otherwise.

methods.name: GetConstructionPoints
methods.summary: Gets current construction points.
methods.remarks: Construction points are like snap points except that they get snapped to even when point osnap is off. Typically, there are only a few construction points while there can be many snap points. For example, when polylines are drawn the start point is a construction point and the other points are snap points.

returns.doc: An array of points.

methods.name: GetPlanarConstraint
methods.signature: public bool GetPlanarConstraint(ref RhinoViewport vp, out Plane plane)

params.name: vp
params.doc: [Missing <param name="vp"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.GetPlanarConstraint(Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport@,Rhino.Geometry.Plane@)"]

params.name: plane
params.doc: [Missing <param name="plane"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.GetPlanarConstraint(Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport@,Rhino.Geometry.Plane@)"]

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.GetPlanarConstraint(Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport@,Rhino.Geometry.Plane@)"]

methods.name: GetSelectedEnumValue<T>
methods.signature: public T GetSelectedEnumValue<T>() where T : struct, new(), IConvertible
methods.summary: Returns the selected enumerated value. Use this in combination with AddOptionEnumList<T>(String, T). This must be called directly after having called a Get method, and having obtained a Option value.

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetBaseClass.GetSelectedEnumValue``1"]

throws.when: Gets thrown if type T is not an enumerated type.
If 0 >= CurrentListOptionIndex or CurrentListOptionIndex > N where N is the number of enumerated values.

methods.name: GetSelectedEnumValueFromSelectionList<T>
methods.signature: public T GetSelectedEnumValueFromSelectionList<T>(IEnumerable<T> selectionList) where T : struct, new(), IConvertible
methods.summary: Returns the selected enumerated value by looking at the list of values from which to select. Use this in combination with AddOptionEnumSelectionList<T>(String, IEnumerable<T>, Int32)

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetBaseClass.GetSelectedEnumValueFromSelectionList``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0})"]

throws.when: Gets thrown if type T is not an enumerated type.
If 0 >= CurrentListOptionIndex or CurrentListOptionIndex > N where N is the number of enumerated values.

methods.name: GetSnapPoints
methods.summary: Gets current snap points.

returns.doc: An array of points.

methods.name: GotDefault
methods.summary: Returns true if user pressed Enter to accept a default point, number, or string set using SetDefaultPoint, SetDefaultNumber, or SetDefaultString.

returns.doc: true if the result if the default point, number or string set. Otherwise, false.

methods.name: InterruptMouseMove
methods.summary: If you have lengthy computations in OnMouseMove() and/or DymanicDraw() overrides, then periodically call InterruptMouseMove() to see if you should interrupt your work because the mouse has moved again.

returns.doc: true if you should interrupt your work; false otherwise.

methods.name: Line2d
methods.summary: Returns two points defining the location in the view window of the 2d line selected in GetPoint::Get2dLine(). (0,0) = upper left corner of window.

returns.doc: An array with two 2D points.

methods.name: Number
methods.summary: Gets a number if GetPoint.Get(), GetObject.GetObjects(), etc., returns GetResult.Number.

returns.doc: The number chosen by the user.

methods.name: NumberPreview
methods.summary: If the user is typing a number, but hasn't pressed Enter, true is returned, along with the number the user typed.

params.name: number
params.doc: The number typed by the user.

returns.doc: true if successful, false otherwise.

methods.name: OnDynamicDraw
methods.summary: Default calls the DynamicDraw event.

params.name: e
params.doc: Current argument for the event.

methods.name: OnMouseDown
methods.summary: Default calls the MouseDown event.

params.name: e
params.doc: Current argument for the event.

methods.name: OnMouseMove
methods.summary: Calls the MouseMove event and can/should be called by overriding implementation.

params.name: e
params.doc: Current argument for the event.

methods.name: OnPostDrawObjects
methods.summary: In the "rare" case that you need to draw some depth buffered geometry during a GetPoint operation, override the OnPostDrawObjects function. NOTE!! Overriding this function comes with a significant performance penalty because the scene needs to be fully regenerated every frame where the standard DynamicDraw event draws temporary decorations (geometry) on top of a static scene.

params.name: e
params.doc: Current argument for the event.

methods.name: Option
methods.summary: (Inherited from GetBaseClass.)

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetBaseClass.Option"]

methods.name: OptionIndex
methods.summary: (Inherited from GetBaseClass.)

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetBaseClass.OptionIndex"]

methods.name: PermitConstraintOptions
methods.summary: Control the availability of the built-in linear, planar, curve, and surface constraint options like "Along", "AlongPerp", "AlongTan", "AlongParallel", "Between", "OnCrv", "OnSrf", ".x", ".y", ".z", ".xy", etc.
methods.remarks: By default, these built-in constraint options are available unless an explicit constraint is added by calling one of the GetPoint::Constrain functions. Calling GetPoint::ClearConstraints automatically enables the built-in constraint options. The built-in constraint options are never visible on the command line and the user must type the complete option name to activate these options.

params.name: permit
params.doc: if true, then the built-in constraint options are automatically available in GetPoint.

methods.name: PermitElevatorMode
methods.summary: Permits the use of the control key to define a line constraint.

params.name: permitMode
params.doc: 0: no elevator modes are permitted 1: fixed plane elevator mode (like the Line command) 2: cplane elevator mode (like object dragging)

methods.name: PermitFromOption
methods.summary: Control the availability of the built-in "From" option. By default, the "From" option is enabled.
methods.remarks: The GetPoint "From" option is never visible on the command line and the user must type the complete option name to activate the "From" option. When the GetPoint "From" snap is enabled, the user set/change the base point during GetPoint by typing "From" and picking a point. A related option is the built-in distance from base point constraint that is can be set before GetPoint is called by passing a value to GetPoint::ConstrainDistanceFromBasePoint or during GetPoint by entering a number.

params.name: permit
params.doc: if true, then the "From" option is automatically available in GetPoint.

methods.name: PermitObjectSnap
methods.summary: By default, object snaps like "end", "near", etc. are controlled by the user. If you want to disable this ability, then call PermitObjectSnap(false).

params.name: permit
params.doc: true to permit snapping to objects.

methods.name: PermitOrthoSnap
methods.summary: Controls availability of ortho snap. Default is true.

params.name: permit
params.doc: if true, then GetPoint pays attention to the Rhino "ortho snap" and "planar snap" settings reported by ModelAidSettings.Ortho and ModelAidSettings.Planar.

methods.name: PermitTabMode
methods.summary: Permits the use of the tab key to define a line constraint.
methods.remarks: By default, use of the tab key is supported.

params.name: permit
params.doc: If true, then the built-in tab key mode is available.

methods.name: PickRectangle
methods.summary: If the get was a GetObjects() and the mouse was used to select the objects, then the returned rectangle has left < right and top < bottom. This rectangle is the Windows GDI screen coordinates of the picking rectangle. RhinoViewport.GetPickXform( pick_rect, pick_xform ) will calculate the picking transformation that was used. In all other cases, left=right=top=bottom=0;

returns.doc: The picking rectangle; or 0 in the specified cases.

methods.name: Point
methods.summary: Gets a point if Get*() returns GetResult.Point.

returns.doc: The point chosen by the user.

methods.name: Point2d
methods.summary: Returns location in view of point in selected in GetPoint::Get() or GetPoint::Get2dPoint(). (0,0) = upper left corner of window.

returns.doc: The location.

methods.name: PointOnBrep
methods.signature: public BrepFace PointOnBrep(out double u, out double v)
methods.summary: Use to determine if point was on a Brep face. If the point was on a Brep face, then the (u,v) are the face parameters for the point.

params.name: u
params.doc: If the point was on a Brep face, then the u parameter.

params.name: v
params.doc: If the point was on a Brep face, then the v parameter.

returns.doc: The Brep face or null if the point was not on a Brep face.

methods.name: PointOnCurve
methods.summary: Use to determine is point was on a curve.

params.name: t
params.doc: If the point was on a curve, then the t is the curve parameter for the point. The point returned by Point() is the same as curve.PointAt(t).

returns.doc: A curve at a specified parameter value.

methods.name: PointOnObject
methods.summary: Call this function to see if the point was on an object. If the point was on an object an ObjRef is returned; otherwise null is returned.

returns.doc: A point object reference.

methods.name: PointOnSurface
methods.signature: public Surface PointOnSurface(out double u, out double v)
methods.summary: Use to determine if point was on a surface. If the point was on a surface, then the (u,v) are the surface parameters for the point. The point returned by Point() is the same as surface.PointAt(u,v).

params.name: u
params.doc: If the point was on a surface, then the u parameter.

params.name: v
params.doc: If the point was on a surface, then the v parameter.

returns.doc: The surface or null if the point was not on a surface.

methods.name: Rectangle2d
methods.summary: Returns the location in the view of the 2d rectangle selected in GetPoint::Get2dRectangle(). rect.left < rect.right and rect.top < rect.bottom (0,0) = upper left corner of window.

returns.doc: The rectangle.

methods.name: Result
methods.summary: Returns result of the Get*() call.

returns.doc: The result of the last Get*() call.

methods.name: SetBasePoint
methods.signature: public void SetBasePoint(Point3d basePoint, bool showDistanceInStatusBar)
methods.summary: Sets a base point used by ortho snap, from snap, planar snap, etc.

params.name: basePoint
params.doc: The new base point.

params.name: showDistanceInStatusBar
params.doc: If true, then the distance from base_point to the current point will be in the status bar distance pane.

methods.name: SetCommandPrompt
methods.summary: Sets prompt message that appears in the command prompt window.

params.name: prompt
params.doc: command prompt message.

methods.name: SetCommandPromptDefault
methods.summary: Sets message that describes what default value will be used if the user presses enter. This description appears in angle brackets <> in the command prompt window. You do not need to provide a default value description unless you explicitly enable AcceptNothing.
methods.remarks: If you have a simple default point, number, or string, it is easier to use SetDefaultPoint, SetDefaultNumber, or SetDefaultString. SetCommandPromptDefault and AcceptNothing can be used for providing more advanced UI behavior.

params.name: defaultValue
params.doc: description of default value.

methods.name: SetCursor
methods.summary: Sets cursor that will be used when Get() is called and snap is not happening.

params.name: cursor
params.doc: [Missing <param name="cursor"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.SetCursor(Rhino.UI.CursorStyle)"]

methods.name: SetDefaultColor
methods.summary: Sets a color as default value that will be returned if the user presses ENTER key during the get.
methods.remarks: Calling SetDefaultColor will automatically handle setting the command prompt default and reacting to the user pressing ENTER. If the user presses ENTER to accept the default color, GetResult.Color is returned and RhinoGet.GotDefault() will return true. Calling SetDefaultColor will clear any previous calls to SetDefaultString or SetDefaultPoint.

params.name: defaultColor
params.doc: value for default color.

methods.name: SetDefaultInteger
methods.summary: Sets a number as default value that will be returned if the user presses ENTER key during the get.
methods.remarks: Calling SetDefaultInteger will automatically handle setting the command prompt default and reacting to the user pressing ENTER. If the user presses ENTER to accept the default integer, GetResult.Number is returned and CRhinoGet.GotDefault() will return true. Calling SetDefaultNumber will clear any previous calls to SetDefaultString or SetDefaultPoint.

params.name: defaultValue
params.doc: value for default number.

methods.name: SetDefaultNumber
methods.summary: Sets a number as default value that will be returned if the user presses ENTER key during the get.
methods.remarks: Calling SetDefaultNumber will automatically handle setting the command prompt default and reacting to the user pressing ENTER. If the user presses ENTER to accept the default number, GetResult.Number is returned and RhinoGet.GotDefault() will return true. Calling SetDefaultNumber will clear any previous calls to SetDefaultString or SetDefaultPoint.

params.name: defaultNumber
params.doc: value for default number.

methods.name: SetDefaultPoint
methods.summary: Sets a point as default value that will be returned if the user presses the ENTER key during the get.
methods.remarks: Calling SetDefaultPoint will automatically handle setting the command prompt default and reacting to the user pressing ENTER. If the user presses enter to accept the default point, GetResult.Point is returned and RhinoGet.GotDefault() will return true. Calling SetDefaultPoint will clear any previous calls to SetDefaultString or SetDefaultNumber.

params.name: point
params.doc: value for default point.

methods.name: SetDefaultString
methods.summary: Sets a string as default value that will be returned if the user presses ENTER key during the get.
methods.remarks: Calling SetDefaultString will automatically handle setting the command prompt default and reacting to the user pressing ENTER. If the user presses ENTER to accept the default string, GetResult.String is returned and RhinoGet.GotDefault() will return true. Calling SetDefaultString will clear any previous calls to SetDefaultNumber or SetDefaultPoint.

params.name: defaultValue
params.doc: value for default string.

methods.name: SetOptionVaries
methods.signature: public void SetOptionVaries(int optionIndex, bool varies)
methods.summary: Sets a command line option value to print "Varies" instead of the regular value.

params.name: optionIndex
params.doc: The option index.

params.name: varies
params.doc: True to print "Varies", false to print the option's current value.

methods.name: SetWaitDuration
methods.summary: Sets the wait duration (in milliseconds) of the getter. If the duration passes without the user making a decision, the GetResult.Timeout code is returned.

params.name: milliseconds
params.doc: Number of milliseconds to wait.

methods.name: StringResult
methods.summary: Gets a string if GetPoint.Get(), GetObject.GetObjects(), etc., returns GetResult.String.

returns.doc: The string chosen by the user.

params.name: basePoint
params.doc: [Missing <param name="basePoint"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.TryGetBasePoint(Rhino.Geometry.Point3d@)"]

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.Input.Custom.GetPoint.TryGetBasePoint(Rhino.Geometry.Point3d@)"]

methods.name: Vector
methods.summary: Gets a direction if Get*() returns GetResult.Point (Set by some digitizers, but in general it's (0,0,0).

returns.doc: The vector chosen by the user.

methods.name: View
methods.summary: Gets a view the user clicked in during GetPoint.Get(), GetObject.GetObjects(), etc.

returns.doc: The view chosen by the user.

properties.name: DynamicDrawColor
properties.summary: Color used by CRhinoGetPoint::DynamicDraw to draw the current point and the line from the base point to the current point.

properties.name: FullFrameRedrawDuringGet
properties.summary: In the "RARE" case that you need to draw some depth buffered geometry during a Get() operation, setting this value to true will force entire frames to be redrawn while the user moves the mouse. This allows DisplayPipeline events to be triggered as well as OnPostDrawObjects NOTE!! Setting this value to true comes with a significant performance penalty because the scene needs to be fully regenerated every frame where the standard DynamicDraw event draws temporary decorations (geometry) on top of a static scene.

properties.name: OsnapEventType
properties.summary: Gets the type of object snap used to obtain the point.

properties.name: Tag
properties.summary: Gets or sets an arbitrary object that can be attached to this GetPoint instance. Useful for passing some/ information that you may need in a DynamicDraw event since you can get at this Tag from the GetPointDrawEventArgs.

events.name: DynamicDraw
events.summary: Event to use if you want to dynamically draw things as the mouse/digitizer moves. Every time the mouse moves, DynamicDraw will be called once per viewport. The calls to DynamicDraw happen AFTER the call to MouseMove. If you are drawing anything that takes a long time, periodically call InterruptMouseMove() to see if you should stop.

events.name: MouseDown
events.summary: Called during Get2dRectangle, Get2dLine, and GetPoint(..,true) when the mouse down event for the initial point occurs. This function is not called during ordinary point getting because the mouse down event terminates an ordinary point get and returns a GetResult.Point result.

events.name: MouseMove
events.summary: Called every time the mouse moves. MouseMove is called once per mouse move and is called BEFORE any calls to OnDynamicDraw. If you are doing anything that takes a long time, periodically call InterruptMouseMove() to see if you should stop. If the view is such that the 2d screen point can't be mapped to a 3d point, the 'point' argument will be Unset.

events.name: PostDrawObjects
events.summary: Same as the DisplayPipeline.PostDrawObjects, but only works during the operation of the Get() function. NOTE: You must set FullFrameRedrawDuringGet to true in order for this event to be called.
